name: NYC BACUN EGGIN CHEEZE
intro: Bacon, egg and cheese are a New York City breakfast staple. This sandwich is served in local bodegas all over New York City, and just like the name, it is scrambled eggs with melted cheese and bacon on a roll, or a hero if you’re feeling hungry. Some people eat it with mayonnaise, ketchup, salt and pepper, just as I used to. This sandwich used to cost only $3 to $5, and it was a quick meal to get before school or work. Local bodega guys always hold it down, but you have to make sure you know how to order it: You simply ask for “baconeggincheese” as if it’s one word. You could add that you want everything on it, or you could be specific if you’re not into every topping. If there’s anything that feels like New York City, I would say it’s bacon, egg and cheese. For my recipe, we use alternatives: tofu for the egg, king oyster mushroom for the bacon—and vegan cheese. This is different from the traditional sandwich, but very similar in taste. Ya Hurrddddd. Now, let’s get it poppin’.

ingredients.item: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

method: Prepare the bacun and set aside.

For the tofu egg, begin by gently pressing out all the liquid from the tofu. Rub the tofu gently with the black salt. Heat the oil in a skillet (choose one with a lid, though you’re not using it yet) over medium heat and grill the tofu in the oil. Cook for 4 to 5 minutes per side while pressing. The black salt will give it an eggy taste and smell.

Place the sliced vegan cheese on top of the tofu and add the water to the pan. Cover with a lid to melt the cheese. Remove the tofu egg and cheese and slice the block of tofu into four square pieces. Slice open the bread rolls and toast in the pan for about 2 minutes.
Place two pieces of tofu egg and cheese into each slice roll, topped with a few pieces of bacun, then add salt, pepper or other condiments of your liking. Enjoy this beloved NYC breakfast!
